pi_items.metadata_value: KSFO ILS RWY 28R approach is used in task description
pi_items.verified_value: KSFO Runway 28R exists as 11,870 ft × 200 ft asphalt runway with Category III ILS/DME equipment
pi_items.source: FlightAware runway data, Wikipedia KSFO article, GlobalAir.com runway specifications

pi_items.metadata_value: ARCHI is an Intermediate Fix (IF) on KSFO 28R approach
pi_items.verified_value: ARCHI is a real Initial Approach Fix (IAF) at KSFO for runways 28L/R approaches
pi_items.source: Multiple aviation forums and procedure documentation mentioning ARCHI at SFO-092/25 DME

pi_items.metadata_value: DUMBA is Final Approach Fix (FAF) on KSFO 28R approach
pi_items.verified_value: DUMBA is a real waypoint for KSFO 28R approaches, located near Dumbarton Bridge
pi_items.source: Search results referencing aircraft joining final approach at DUMBA for runways 28L/R

pi_items.verified_value: 111.7 MHz (Channel 54) is the correct ILS frequency for KSFO runway 28R
pi_items.source: Multiple aviation databases including airport-data.com and search results on KSFO ILS specifications

pi_items.verified_value: The correct identifier for 111.7 MHz at KSFO 28R is I-GWQ (LOC/DME), not I-SFO
pi_items.source: Search results explicitly stating 'ILS frequency 111.7 is on Channel 54 at San Francisco International Airport, with the identifier I-GWQ'

pi_items.verified_value: ARCHI is at 8000A feet (at or above 8,000 feet) on KSFO approaches
pi_items.source: Navigraph forum discussing ARCHI waypoint at SFO-092/25 DME with altitude restriction of 8000A

pi_items.verified_value: DUMBA appears at 3500 feet on KSFO NORCAL Approach briefing strip; conflicting data on exact FAF altitude
pi_items.source: Search results mentioning DUMBA at 3500 feet on approach charts, though exact FAF altitude constraints vary by procedure

pi_items.verified_value: KSFO airport elevation is confirmed as 13 feet MSL
pi_items.source: Multiple aviation sources including AirNav, SkyVector, Wikipedia confirming KSFO elevation of 13 feet

pi_items.verified_value: KSFO runway 28R glideslope angle is 3.00 degrees
pi_items.source: NTSB document 'Attachment 11 - SFO Runway 28R Approach Charts' confirming 3.00 degree glideslope

pi_items.verified_value: Data file is created synthetically by setup_task.sh; not downloaded from external sources
pi_items.source: Direct examination of setup_task.sh showing programmatic creation of iap_data.txt with embedded data

privileged_info_summary: This task uses a synthetic aviation dataset based on real KSFO (San Francisco International Airport) runway 28R procedures. Verified facts: KSFO runway 28R exists as a real 11,870 ft runway with Category III ILS equipment. ARCHI and DUMBA are real waypoints on KSFO approaches. ILS frequency 111.7 MHz is correct for KSFO 28R. Airport elevation is 13 feet. Glideslope angle is 3.00 degrees. However, the synthetic altitudes differ from real procedure data: the task uses 4000 feet for ARCHI (real: 8000A feet) and 1800 feet for DUMBA (conflicting data: 3500 feet on NORCAL Approach). The ILS identifier in the setup script (I-SFO) is incorrect; the correct identifier is I-GWQ.
